commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=155bd02c894bbaa8b8bbe004948de67d71b03c83)
commands.review_summary:
The PR hardens tool-call handling by tightening `sessions_send` target validation, adding repeated tool-error loop termination, disabling default plain-text tool-call promotion, and changing tool-capability defaults for unknown/OpenRouter models.

PR surface: Source +53, Tests +254. Total +307 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection is enough to reproduce the introduced regressions: existing provider wrapper call sites become pass-through, `sessions_send({ label, agentId })` now fails validation, and providers without `compat.supportsTools` lose tool payloads.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof plus maintainer-visible compatibility fixes before any automation or merge path is safe.

target.title: fix(agents): harden tool-call handling against A2A/model tool-call poisoning
target.body:
## Summary

Hardens four runtime paths that together let a non-tool-capable or misbehaving model drive an agent into an unbounded **invalid tool-call loop**. In our deployment this surfaced as an agent-to-agent `sessions_send` retry storm: a model emitted tool-shaped text / repeatedly-invalid `sessions_send` calls, the runtime kept feeding the validation errors back into context, and the agent never terminated.

Each change is defense-in-depth — the loop required several lenient behaviors lining up.

## Changes

### 1. `sessions_send` — strict, terminal target selectors
`src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.ts`
- The three target selectors (`sessionKey`, `label`, `agentId`) are now **strictly mutually exclusive**. Previously only `sessionKey + label` was rejected; `sessionKey + agentId` silently ignored `agentId`, and `label + agentId` was accepted. Mixed selectors now return one clear error.
- All error/forbidden returns are marked **terminal + `isError`** (`terminate: true`, `details.isError: true`) so the agent loop stops re-prompting on the same bad call instead of looping.

### 2. Agent loop — poison-loop guard + error propagation
`packages/agent-core/src/agent-loop.ts`
- Track repeated **identical `isError` tool results** within a turn and terminate the inner tool loop after the second identical failure.
- Honor `result.details.isError` when finalizing a tool call so tool-reported errors actually count as errors.

### 3. Plain-text tool-call promotion off by default
`src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-shared.ts`
- `createPlainTextToolCallCompatWrapper` now delegates straight to the underlying stream; promotion of standalone bracketed text into tool calls is no longer applied by default. `wrapPlainTextToolCallStream` is exported so providers/tests can opt in explicitly.

### 4. Conservative tool-capability defaults
`src/agents/model-tool-support.ts`
- Unknown/custom providers default to **tools OFF**. Direct providers with native tool support (`openai`, `openai-codex`, `anthropic`, `google`) default **ON**. An explicit `compat.supportsTools` still wins either way.

`extensions/openrouter/index.ts`
- Dynamic OpenRouter models default `compat.supportsTools=false` unless the OpenRouter catalog reports tools support.

## Tests
Added/updated regression tests for each path:
- `src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts` — mixed-selector rejection + terminal/isError.
- `packages/agent-core/src/agent-loop.test.ts` — repeated-error poison-loop termination.
- `src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-shared.test.ts` — compat wrapper no longer promotes.
- `src/agents/model-tool-support.test.ts` — provider-aware defaults.
- `extensions/openrouter/index.test.ts` — dynamic model tools default off.

Targeted suites pass locally (172 tests). `oxfmt --check` clean on all touched files; `tsgo` core-test typecheck clean for the touched files (remaining tsgo errors are pre-existing and unrelated: `net-policy/ip.ts`, UI `playwright`/`dompurify` module resolution).

## Notes
This corresponds to an emergency local hotfix applied to a running deployment; this PR is the upstream-quality version of those changes.
